summary: First testing iteration for TEKOSECURE MVP FASE 1. Backend (pytest, 8/8) and frontend (Playwright, 19/19) all pass. Login, dashboard metrics, alert closing with Supabase persistence, filters, NVRs grid, realtime layout, logout, protected routes and responsive mobile layout all work as spec'd.
action_items: Consider: seed data was slightly altered by tests. test_close_alert_flow closes one active alert (spec allowed). Started with 6 ACTIVE, now 4 remain visible (one closed via API test, one closed via UI test). Re-seed with 'python3 /app/scripts/seed_supabase.py' before demo if needed.
Minor UX: SOC clock shows system date 2026-07-20; 'ATAQUES HOY' correctly reflects same-day filter (7 attacks today from seed data). No action needed unless business rules require a fixed date.
NOTE: /api/actions/block-ip is MOCKED (simulated response, Phase 2 will call fail2ban) - as documented in the review request.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is compact and well structured (~112 lines). Uses env vars, httpx async, Pydantic validation, proper 401 handling. Good.
supabase.js uses REACT_APP_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY correctly. autoRefreshToken enabled.
DashboardHome.jsx (~350 lines) is at edge but acceptable for MVP; could be split into hooks (useAlerts, useNvrs) later.
AlertsPage/NvrPage do client-side filtering — fine for MVP scale (9 NVRs, dozens of alerts). If dataset grows, move filters to Supabase server-side.
Realtime channels are properly cleaned up on unmount (supabase.removeChannel).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. 2 active alerts closed during testing (one via backend API test, one via UI). Re-seed via 'python3 /app/scripts/seed_supabase.py' if needed before demo.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All backend endpoints use /api prefix. Supabase project 'fsucygjqzskwtnynvgob' has anon key in .env (starts with sb_publishable_). Auth JWT obtained via POST /auth/v1/token?grant_type=password with anon key in apikey header. Tables in use: 'attacks' (id, status, severity, source_ip, attack_type, created_at) and 'hikvision_events' (nvr_id, nvr_name, nvr_ip, status, location, created_at). NVR list is derived by taking latest event per nvr_id. NVR id=5 (Zona Franca Global NVR1) is OFFLINE. If dashboard metrics show 0, wait ~3-5s for async Supabase fetch to complete. Frontend uses CRA hot reload. Tests do not need to clean up; TEST_ prefix wasn't needed since we only mutate existing seed data by closing 1 alert.
